title: Leucanthemum catananche
intro: Leucanthemum catananche, also known as Cat's Ear or White Hawk's Beard, is a flowering plant species in the daisy family (Asteraceae), native to the Mediterranean region. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health issues, including inflammation, fever, and respiratory problems, due to its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents of Leucanthemum catananche include flavonoids, sesquiterpenes, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. The most common herbal preparations made from Leucanthemum catananche include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various health conditions, including bronchitis, coughs, and skin infections.
